channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court to hear plea over distance of alternate Friday namaz site near Bhojshala on July 29
item.description: The Supreme Court has agreed to hear on July 29 a plea filed by the Muslim side regarding the implementation of its interim directions in the long-running Bhojshala dispute in Dhar, Madhya Pradesh. Du...

item.title: KPMG suspends partner over confidential document mishandling
item.description: KPMG has suspended its former chief operating officer Eileen Hoggett from the firm's partnership following an internal investigation. The decision comes after the firm discovered she had stored printe...

item.title: Study Reveals Canada's Cost of Happiness at $155,850
item.description: A study conducted by digital financial services provider Remitly, based on research from Purdue University, calculates the cost of happiness in different countries. Adjusted for local purchasing power...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Addresses Terrorism and Regional Security at Balochistan Workshop
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif addressed the Balochistan National Workshop, emphasizing India's direct involvement in terrorism within Pakistan. He accused India of sponsoring terrorist activities and ...

item.title: Alleged Kingpin Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala Faces Attempted Murder Charges in Johannesburg High Court
item.description: The attempted murder trial of Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala, a prominent taxi boss, media personality, and musician, continues in the Johannesburg high court. Matlala and co-accused are facing charges relate...

item.title: Mumbai Police Driver Removed Over Threats to Protesters
item.description: The Mumbai Police has removed a driver from his post after a video surfaced showing him allegedly threatening youth protesters with drug charges. The incident, which gained traction on social media, i...

item.title: Pulse Asia Survey Reveals Top Priorities for Marcos' Fifth SONA
item.description: A recent Pulse Asia Survey highlights the key priorities Filipinos expect from President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s fifth State of the Nation Address (SONA) in 2026. The survey, conducted amid the ongoing...

item.title: Delhi Police Clarifies No Death of CJP Founder Abhijeet Dipke, Debunks Fake News
item.description: The Delhi Police has clarified that there is no truth to the fake newspaper clipping circulating online about the death of Abhijeet Dipke, the founder of the Chhatrapati Shivaji Pageant (CJP). The inc...

item.title: JD Vance Faces Internal GOP Attacks Over Foreign Policy Stances
item.description: The article details an early and aggressive online revolt within the Republican Party targeting Vice President JD Vance, who is considering a 2028 presidential bid. The Washington Post reports that Va...

item.title: Rayner Opposes Rent Controls, Highlights Impact of New Legislation
item.description: Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has stated that Prime Minister Andy Burnham is not pursuing rent controls in England. She emphasized that the Renters' Rights Act, implemented in May 2026, has already ...

item.title: Economic Benefits of the Gordie Howe Bridge: Boosting Trade and Efficiency
item.description: The Gordie Howe International Bridge, connecting Windsor, Ontario, and Detroit, is set to open after years of planning and construction. This project aims to alleviate congestion in the Windsor-Detroi...

item.title: ICC Votes on Dismissing Prosecutor Khan Amid Allegations and Political Pressures
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) is set to vote on July 24 regarding the dismissal of chief prosecutor Karim Khan over allegations of sexual misconduct. Khan, 56, denies any wrongdoing, with his...

item.title: A Search and Rescue Specialist's Journey Through Taiwan's Mountains
item.description: The article details the experiences of a search and rescue specialist in Taiwan's remote mountainous regions. The author recounts their journey into the mountains, driven by a passion for exploration ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz unveils Pakistan's largest data centre and AI cloud Sky47 in Islamabad
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif inaugurated Pakistan's largest data centre and AI cloud, Sky47, in Islamabad, emphasizing its role as a cornerstone for technological advancement. The facility, built in ...

item.title: UN Appeals For Evacuation Of 6,000 Stranded Sailors In Hormuz Strait
item.description: The United Nations has called on countries involved in the Middle East conflict to help evacuate and repatriate around 6,000 sailors stuck in the Strait of Hormuz. The UN human rights chief, Volker Tu...

item.title: ODM Supporters' 2027 Presidential Preferences Revealed in TIFA Poll
item.description: A new TIFA survey has revealed that 44% of Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) supporters prefer Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna as their preferred presidential candidate for the 2027 general election, whil...

item.title: South Africa Rejects Ghanaian Petition to Investigate Migrant Violence at ICC
item.description: South Africa has rejected a petition filed by two Ghanaian nationals at the International Criminal Court (ICC) seeking an investigation into attacks on foreign migrants. The petition, submitted to the...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Unveils Pakistan's Largest Data Center and AI Cloud in Islamabad
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif inaugurated the Sky47 AI cloud and data centre in Islamabad, marking a significant step in Pakistan's technological advancement. The facility, described as the country's ...

item.title: 18-year-old woman's murder in UP's Shamli linked to parents' opposition to her relationship
item.description: An 18-year-old woman, Hina, was allegedly murdered by her parents in Shamli district of Uttar Pradesh over their opposition to her relationship with a youth. The police, based on a complaint from her ...

item.title: Labor avoids gas tax commitment amid One Nation pressure and public demand for industry reform
item.description: The article discusses the Australian Labor Party's decision to abandon plans to enshrine a gas tax in its platform during its national conference. Despite initial hopes to address calls for a fairer r...
